layout_description:
Option: OPT-1
Slide description: TWO-COLUMN SIDE-BY-SIDE LAYOUT with equal-width columns separated by a consistent gutter. The slide uses a 4-column × 6-row grid structure with all containers snapping to grid boundaries. Both tables are RECREATE containers and receive identical visual treatment using Section 4 Option C (Flat Geometry Table) from the design guidelines — formal financial data tables with no filled header bars, accent tabs beneath column headers, light grey row label cells, and thin dashed grey separators between rows. The layout maximizes horizontal space utilization by allocating roughly 40% width to each table with a 2–3% gutter between them, leaving small margins at slide edges. Vertical space is fully utilized: tables extend from just below the title area to near the bottom of the slide, filling approximately 85% of the available vertical space. The slide uses content-aware spacing: padding is minimal (dense content with 18+ rows across two tables), and dashed separator lines provide visual rhythm without consuming vertical space. All row sections within each table use equal flexGrow to distribute height evenly, preventing top-stacking. The layout enforces horizontal alignment precision: corresponding rows in both tables share identical top and bottom edges, and all separator lines align horizontally across both columns.

Blueprint:
  Orientation: left-to-right
  Regions: 2
  Allocation: [0.4, 0.46]

Containers:
  C0 (LEFT COLUMN — Bookings Table): Left column table occupying approximately 40% of slide width, positioned at the left edge of the content zone with a small left margin (3–5% from slide edge). Height extends from just below the title area (top_pct ~14%) to near the bottom of the slide (~66% height), filling the available vertical space. This is a RECREATE container using Section 4 Option C (Flat Geometry Table) visual treatment. The table has 7 columns (Region, Customer, Country, Q1, Jan, Feb, Mar) and approximately 18 data rows organized into two regional sections (NA and Intl). Column headers are plain bold text in brand dark color, left-aligned, with no filled background. Directly beneath each header label sits a small accent tab (brand primary color, very thin height, narrow width relative to column, top corners slightly rounded, left-aligned with the header text, sitting fully below the text and never overlapping it). Immediately below and touching each accent tab is a thin solid horizontal line in the same brand color, spanning that column's width with small white gutters between columns (not a single full-width line). The first column (Region) contains row label cells: light grey filled rectangles (no border, no rounded corners, full column width, most of the row height) with bold text in brand dark color, left-aligned with a small left inset, vertically centered. These label cells span multiple rows for each region (NA spans ~10 rows, Intl spans ~8 rows). Other columns contain numerical data: plain text on white background (no fill), brand text color, horizontally centered within each cell, vertically centered to align with the row label cell. Rows are separated by thin dashed grey horizontal lines spanning the full table width. The 'Total NA' and 'Total Intl' rows receive bold text treatment to distinguish them as subtotals. The 'Total' row at the bottom receives bold text and sits below a slightly heavier dashed separator. Font size is reduced to fit the dense 18-row content within the available height — target range is medium body font, ensuring all text fits without overflow. All row sections use equal flexGrow to distribute height evenly across the table, preventing content from bunching at the top. Highlighted cells (green fills visible in source) preserve their exact highlight colors as accent fills behind the cell text. Column widths are content-weighted: Region column sized to fit 'Intl' text, Customer column sized to fit longest customer name without wrapping, Country column narrow (2-letter codes), numerical columns (Q1, Jan, Feb, Mar) share remaining space equally.
  C1 (RIGHT COLUMN — Revenues Table): Right column table occupying approximately 46% of slide width, positioned at the right side of the content zone with a small right margin (3–5% from slide edge) and a 2–3% gutter separating it from the left column. Height extends from just below the title area (top_pct ~12.5%) to near the bottom of the slide (~66.5% height), matching the vertical extent of the left column table. This is a RECREATE container using Section 4 Option C (Flat Geometry Table) visual treatment, identical to the left column. The table has 8 columns (Region, Customer, Country, Q1, Jan, Feb, Mar, and an unlabeled rightmost column visible in source) and approximately 18 data rows organized into two regional sections (NA and Intl). Column headers are plain bold text in brand dark color, left-aligned, with no filled background. Directly beneath each header label sits a small accent tab (brand primary color, very thin height, narrow width relative to column, top corners slightly rounded, left-aligned with the header text, sitting fully below the text). Immediately below and touching each accent tab is a thin solid horizontal line in the same brand color, spanning that column's width with small white gutters between columns. The first column (Region) contains row label cells: light grey filled rectangles (no border, no rounded corners, full column width, most of the row height) with bold text in brand dark color, left-aligned with a small left inset, vertically centered. These label cells span multiple rows for each region (NA spans ~10 rows, Intl spans ~8 rows). Other columns contain numerical data: plain text on white background, brand text color, horizontally centered within each cell, vertically centered to align with the row label cell. Rows are separated by thin dashed grey horizontal lines spanning the full table width, positioned at exactly the same vertical positions as the separator lines in the left column table (horizontal alignment precision rule B5). The 'Total NA' and 'Total Intl' rows receive bold text treatment. The 'Total' row at the bottom receives bold text and sits below a slightly heavier dashed separator. Font size matches the left column exactly (within-slide font uniformity rule C6) — medium body font reduced to fit dense content. All row sections use equal flexGrow values matching the left column, ensuring that corresponding rows in both tables share identical top and bottom edges (horizontal alignment rule B2). Highlighted cells (green fills visible in source) preserve their exact highlight colors. Column widths are content-weighted: Region column sized to fit 'Intl', Customer column sized to fit longest name, Country column narrow, numerical columns share remaining space equally. The title text 'Revenues - 2026' visible in the source image is rendered as a bold header row above the column headers, spanning the full table width, with brand dark text on white background (no filled bar), left-aligned.
  SC-BOTTOM (DECORATIVE BACKGROUND LAYER): Inactive. The slide is well-filled by the two content tables, which together cover approximately 86% of the usable slide area (2 tables × 40–46% width × 66% height). No decorative background layer is needed — adding one would violate the accent color budget (rule C3) and provide no visual grouping benefit. The tables are visually unified through their identical design treatment (Flat Geometry Table style) and precise horizontal alignment of row separators.
  SC-TOP (OVERLAY CONNECTOR LAYER): Inactive. No cross-container connectors, arrows, or overlay elements are needed. The two tables are independent data presentations with no semantic relationship requiring visual connection. All content is contained within the two table containers.

_reference_violations.description: Both tables (C0 and C1) occupy significantly less than the specified vertical space. The layout description specifies tables should extend from ~12-14% top to ~80% bottom (66-68% height), filling approximately 85% of available vertical space. In the rendered sketch, both tables end at approximately 55-60% of slide height, leaving a large empty white band (>20% of slide area) below the tables.

Both C0 (Bookings Table) and C1 (Revenues Table) are severely under-filled vertically. The table content (18 rows including headers and totals) occupies approximately 35-40% of the available slide height, while the containers were allocated 66-68% height. The empty space below the tables is larger than the space occupied by the table content itself. This creates a massive empty band at the bottom of the slide.

C1 (Revenues Table) is missing one column. The source slide shows 8 columns (Region, Customer, Country, Q1, Jan, Feb, Mar, and an unlabeled rightmost column). The sketch shows only 7 columns (Region, Customer, Country, Q1, Jan, Feb, Mar). The rightmost unlabeled column visible in the source is absent from the sketch.

_reference_critique:
CRITICAL VIOLATIONS FOUND:

1. SPACE UTILIZATION & CONTAINER FILL (C0, C1): Both tables are severely under-filled vertically. The layout description specifies tables extending from ~12-14% top_pct to ~80% bottom (66-68% height), filling approximately 85% of available vertical space. The rendered sketch shows both tables ending at approximately 55-60% of slide height, leaving a massive empty white band (>20% of slide area) below the tables. The table content occupies only ~35-40% of the slide height when it should occupy 66-68%.

SVG_SKETCHER MUST:
- Increase the height allocation for both C0 and C1 containers from current ~45-50% to the specified 66-68% height.
- Increase row heights proportionally to fill the allocated container space. The 18 rows (including headers, data rows, subtotals, and total) must distribute across the full 66-68% height.
- Increase font size for all table text (headers, data cells, row labels) to fill the expanded row heights. Current font appears to be ~10-11pt; increase toward 13-14pt range to achieve proper fill ratio.
- Ensure the bottom border of both tables sits at approximately 80% of slide height (not 55-60% as currently rendered).

2. CONTENT FIDELITY - MISSING COLUMN (C1): The Revenues table (C1) is missing one column. The source slide clearly shows 8 columns in the Revenues table (Region, Customer, Country, Q1, Jan, Feb, Mar, plus an unlabeled rightmost column). The sketch renders only 7 columns.

SVG_SKETCHER MUST:
- Add the missing 8th column to C1 (Revenues Table). Inspect the source slide to identify the content of the rightmost unlabeled column and render it in the sketch.
- Adjust column widths proportionally to accommodate 8 columns instead of 7 within the allocated ~46% slide width for C1.

All other aspects (alignment, row count, data accuracy, visual treatment) are acceptable.
